title: Executive Order 9863—Designating Public International Organizations Entitled to Enjoy Certain Privileges Exemptions, and Immunities

flags.justification: Applies existing statutory designation authority (International Organizations Immunities Act) to name specific organizations; no new authority is created.
No ambiguity is created by this designation action.
Single coherent action: designating three named organizations under one statutory authority.
This grants privileges rather than creating obligations or a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.

Standing-procedural-framework category applies: the order explicitly 'supplements' three prior similar orders, demonstrating an ongoing pattern of periodic amendment as new organizations are recognized, rather than a static, unreviewed grant.
The order explicitly preserves other privileges 'which such organizations may have acquired or may acquire by treaty or Congressional action' — no existing mechanism is removed.
Grants privileges rather than creating an ongoing compliance regime.
A routine, narrow application of an established statutory designation process.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.

The order's entire purpose is to grant the immunity in question under an enabling statute designed for exactly that purpose; this is not an exemption undermining some other regulatory scheme's stated purpose, so the flag's concept does not map.
